definition: I2S plants a chunk-type FOURCC to enter a specific PNG handler, then also manufactures a chunk-length (or inner-field length) that fires an inequality gate inside that handler.

authoring_contract.goal: ONE parameterized template.c isolating this cluster's shared mechanism, with exactly ONE compile-time -D knob = the program-feature axis. params.json:parameter MUST name that macro; scan_values sweep it; fuzzers = involved_fuzzers; expected_direction = '<winner> > <loser>'.
authoring_contract.isolate: No partial-match coverage edges unless the mechanism IS a gradient (VP families). Keep the gate the only objective.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver entry for the gAMA length-vs-4 comparison and splices a non-4 value into the input bytes at the chunk-length offset, deterministically producing length!=4; naive has no CMP introspection and keeps length==4 by chance.
signature.one_line: I2S splices non-4 value into gAMA chunk-length field to flip inequality gate

analysis.summary_one_line: A gAMA chunk whose declared length field is not exactly 4 takes the invalid-length error branch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The 4-byte big-endian length field preceding a gAMA chunk (input offsets ~0x21-0x24, immediately before the 'gAMA' fourcc at 0x25-0x28) must hold a value other than 4, so png_handle_gAMA receives length != 4 and takes the true (invalid) branch at pngrutil.c:1136.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: cmplog seeds reach the true branch (resolved=10/10); the per-offset byte diff shows winner inputs carry non-4 length bytes immediately ahead of the gAMA tag (e.g. offset 0x23 holds 0x80/0x01 in 5 winner seeds where the loser is uniformly 0x00), forcing length != 4. I2SRandReplace substitutes the logged comparand for the gAMA length CMP into the matching input offset.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive never resolves (blocked=10/10); its blocking seeds keep the canonical length value at the gAMA length offsets (offset 0x23 is 0x00 across all 10 loser samples and offset 0x24 is 0x04), so length stays at the valid value 4 and the false branch is taken. Without I2S, havoc rarely hits the exact length offset with a non-4 value that still parses to the gAMA chunk.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver entry for the length-vs-4 comparison and splices a differing operand into the input bytes at the offset that fed the comparison, deterministically producing length != 4; naive has only havoc/token mutators and no CMP introspection.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the gAMA length check whose globally blocked side is the true branch.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
Source line:    if (length != 4)
Globally blocked side: T  (true branch)

evidence_trail.claim: The gAMA fourcc sits at input offset 0x25-0x28, so the preceding 4 bytes are the chunk length field.
Winner inputs carry non-zero length bytes at offset 0x23 where losers are uniformly zero, forcing length != 4.
cmplog resolves the true branch in every trial while naive blocks in every trial.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
winner: resolved=10/10  blocked=0  unreached=0
  loser:  resolved=0/10  blocked=10  unreached=0

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog adds I2SRandReplace on top of naive's mutators, substituting a logged CMP operand into matching input offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S and relies only on havoc plus token mutators.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx resolves the branch in all trials with much lower blocked duration than naive.
naive_ctx folds the call context into the edge-map index so the same edge is recorded at different slots by call path.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
folds it into the edge-map index, so the same basic-block edge is
recorded at different map slots depending on the call path that
reached it

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx's new-coverage signal is a new (call-context, edge) pair rather than a bare edge.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
a "new bucket" is a new
(call-context, edge) pair rather than a bare edge

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic gAMA-length harness in which cmplog fails to beat naive at flipping the 4-byte length field off the value 4 (no cmplog>naive divergence), which would refute the I2S-substitution attribution for pair 1.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The exact input offset of the length field is inferred from the gAMA fourcc position and the concentrated byte-diff at 0x23; the harness may carry leading offsets that shift the true length-field offset slightly.

signature.mechanism_summary: vpc's I2SRandReplace logs the cHRM length-vs-32 comparison and splices a non-32 length into the chunk-length field, deterministically flipping the gate; value_profile's CMP_MAP distance-bucket feedback alone cannot manufacture this precise length deviation.
signature.one_line: I2S splices non-32 length into cHRM chunk-length field for inequality gate

analysis.summary_one_line: A cHRM chunk whose declared length field is any value other than 32, taking the length-mismatch error branch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A cHRM chunk (FOURCC 0x63 0x48 0x52 0x4d) must be present and reached, AND its 4-byte big-endian length field must hold a value != 32, so png_handle_cHRM takes the true side of `if (length != 32)` at pngrutil.c:1256.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves 10/10 and hits the true side 16 times (DIVERGENT BRANCHES L1256 W T=16); its winning seeds carry the literal cHRM FOURCC bytes (offset 0x35-0x38 'cHRM') and reach the length compare, and I2S substitution can place a chunk-length value that is not 32 into the 4-byte length field that this exact compare reads.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: value_profile blocks 8/10 and never reaches the true side (DIVERGENT BRANCHES L1256 L T=0); without I2SRandReplace it only ever falls through with length==32 (the false side, T=0 F=12), so it cannot manufacture the precise length!=32 condition at this gate and stalls 20.60h.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace on top of value_profile's CMP_MAP feedback; I2S logs the (length, 32) compare operands and splices a non-32 length into the input bytes that feed `if (length != 32)`, deterministically flipping the gate to its true side, which value_profile's distance-bucket feedback alone cannot accomplish.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the length-mismatch compare in png_handle_cHRM whose true side is globally blocked.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
Source line:    if (length != 32)
Globally blocked side: T  (true branch)

evidence_trail.claim: The single decisive pair is value_profile_cmplog over value_profile with an I2S technique delta.
The winner hits the true side 16 times while the loser never hits it.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L1256  T=16 F=12  T=0 F=12  if (length != 32)  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Winning seeds carry the literal cHRM chunk FOURCC bytes near offset 0x35.
The chunk-type bytes at offsets 0x35-0x38 vary widely in the winner including the cHRM signature, while the loser is essentially fixed.
value_profile lacks the I2SRandReplace mutator that vpc adds.
value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace on top of the havoc/token stack.
The loser blocks 8/10 and stalls for 20.60h while the winner resolves 10/10.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic cHRM-style harness with `if (length != 32)` where value_profile resolves the length!=32 true side as fast as value_profile_cmplog (no I2S advantage), showing CMP_MAP distance feedback alone flips this exact-value gate.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The byte-diff does not isolate the 4-byte length field offset that feeds this compare, so the claim that I2S substitutes the length operand (rather than I2S merely planting the cHRM FOURCC to reach the gate) is inferred from source structure, not a pinpointed length-byte divergence.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace substitutes the logged 'sRGB' chunk-type FOURCC into the input so the dispatcher enters png_handle_sRGB, then mutates the chunk-length field away from 1; naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and cannot place the FOURCC or produce length!=1 together.
signature.one_line: I2S inserts sRGB FOURCC to reach handler then produces length!=1 inequality

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must carry the literal 'sRGB' chunk-type FOURCC (so png_handle_sRGB is reached) with a chunk-length field not equal to 1.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The 4-byte chunk-type tag at offset 0x35-0x38 must equal the ASCII FOURCC 'sRGB' (73 52 47 42) so the PNG chunk dispatcher routes into png_handle_sRGB, after which the 4-byte big-endian chunk-length field at 0x31-0x34 is anything other than 1, taking the `length != 1` true side.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: cmplog/vpc winning seeds carry the exact 'sRGB' tag (offset 0x35-0x38 = 73 52 47 42) and reach png_handle_sRGB (W hits 78 vs L hits 24); I2SRandReplace splices the logged 'sRGB' string operand from the chunk-type comparison into the input so the dispatcher matches, then mutates the length away from 1 (W bytes at 0x34 include 28/86/ef besides 01).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive/value_profile have no I2S substitution and must stumble onto both the FOURCC match and a non-1 length by chance; their blocking seeds keep the length low byte fixed at 01 at offset 0x34 (L = 01 x21) so length stays 1 and the true side is never taken (loser resolved 1/10, blocked 9/10).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog and value_profile_cmplog add I2SRandReplace: the chunk-type strcmp/memcmp against "sRGB" is logged into the CmpLogObserver buffer and the mutator substitutes that operand into the input at the matching offset, opening the path to png_handle_sRGB where the length check can then diverge from 1. value_profile alone lacks I2S, so vpc>vp isolates the same I2S substitution.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the true side of `if (length != 1)` in png_handle_sRGB and is globally blocked.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
Source line:    if (length != 1)
Globally blocked side: T  (true branch)

evidence_trail.claim: The 'sRGB' FOURCC chunk-type tag appears at offset 0x35-0x38 in the seeds.
Winner seeds vary the chunk-length low byte at offset 0x34 while loser seeds keep it fixed at 01.
The winner reaches png_handle_sRGB far more than the loser (78 vs 24 hits).
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which substitutes a logged CMP operand into the input at matching offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace, isolating the I2S delta for the vpc>vp pair.
naive_ctx folds call context into the edge-map index so (call-context, edge) pairs are new coverage.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
a "new bucket" is a new
(call-context, edge) pair rather than a bare edge.

evidence_trail.claim: plain naive records only bare-edge hit-count buckets with no context indexing.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
**Feedback**: per-edge hit-count bucket; a new bucket triggers a
corpus-add (LibAFL `MaxMapFeedback` over the edge map).

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx wins 10/10 while naive loses 9/10 blocked, with naive averaging 21.80h blocked.
Loser naive/value_profile seeds keep the length field at 1 and never take the true side (1/10 resolved).

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where the sRGB chunk-type FOURCC is reached without any byte-literal comparison (e.g. dispatch by a runtime-computed index) and cmplog still outpaces naive at taking the length!=1 branch, showing the divergence is not driven by I2S splicing the logged 'sRGB' operand.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The byte-diff shows the length low byte at 0x34 varying in W vs fixed 01 in L, but it does not directly prove I2S spliced the 'sRGB' FOURCC rather than the FOURCC already being present in the corpus seed prefix.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace logs the chunk-type comparison against 'eXIf' and the length<2 comparison, substitutes the 'eXIf' FOURCC into the chunk-type field and places a sub-2 length value, manufacturing both dispatch and the length<2 gate; naive and value_profile cannot achieve either by blind havoc.
signature.one_line: I2S places eXIf FOURCC and sub-2 length to satisfy chunk-dispatch plus inequality

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must carry an 'eXIf' chunk-type tag whose 4-byte chunk-length field is 0 or 1.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A PNG chunk whose 4-byte type field equals the literal 'eXIf' (so OSS_FUZZ_png_handle_eXIf dispatches) AND whose preceding 4-byte big-endian chunk-length field is < 2 (i.e. 0 or 1), forcing the length<2 TRUE branch at pngrutil.c:2047.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner seeds are cmplog/vpc-discovered and reach the blocker at very low wall-clock time (discovered_at=4s), and the per-role overlay marks the length<2 TRUE arm (lines 2048-2052) as winner-only [W]; cmplog/vpc's I2SRandReplace can splice the logged 'eXIf' chunk-type operand and set the length operand directly so dispatch + length<2 are reached, with the branch hit 29 (vpc) / 7 (cmplog) times.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive and value_profile blocked in 10/10 trials taking only the FALSE arm (length>=2, the [L] else-branch at 2054+); without I2S substitution they must stumble on both the exact 'eXIf' type tag and a length field below 2 by blind havoc, which never happened in 23+h.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc add I2SRandReplace which reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer, finds the chunk-type comparison against 'eXIf' (and the length comparison), and substitutes the matching operand into the input bytes; naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace, so the only difference between each winner/loser pair is the I2S technique.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the length<2 test in png_handle_eXIf and the globally blocked (winning) side is TRUE.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
Source line:    if (length < 2)
Globally blocked side: T  (true branch)

evidence_trail.claim: The winner-resolving arm is the length<2 TRUE branch (lines 2048-2052), exercised only by winner seeds.
Both decisive pairs differ solely by the I2S technique.
naive and value_profile blocked this branch in all 10 trials while their I2S-enabled counterparts resolved it.
Winner seeds reach the branch within seconds of fuzzing start.
The loser arm taken in blocking trials is the length>=2 else-branch (2054 onward), marked loser-only.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L2047  T=23 F=0  T=0 F=35  if (length < 2)  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: I2SRandReplace scans the input for byte sequences matching one CMP operand and substitutes the other, the substitution mechanism enabling the winner.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive and value_profile have no I2SRandReplace mutator.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on an 'eXIf' type tag plus a length<2 field where naive/value_profile resolve the length<2 branch as fast as cmplog/vpc (no I2S advantage), which would mean the divergence is not driven by I2S substitution of the chunk-type/length operands.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: Whether the I2S advantage primarily comes from splicing the 'eXIf' chunk-type tag versus the length operand itself is not separable from the byte evidence, since the length field is a runtime-parsed chunk header value that may have no stable input-byte pre-image for I2S to target.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver record of the chunk-name comparison and substitutes the 'sCAL' FOURCC at the matching input offset, manufacturing the chunk-type literal that routes to png_handle_sCAL; once dispatched, a sub-4 length field satisfies length<4.
signature.one_line: I2S writes sCAL FOURCC to dispatch handler, then sub-4 length fires inequality

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must contain an sCAL chunk whose 4-byte big-endian length field is < 4, hitting the short-chunk reject path.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A well-formed PNG carrying an sCAL chunk (chunk-type FOURCC 's','C','A','L') placed after IHDR and before IDAT, where the chunk's 4-byte big-endian length field holds a value strictly less than 4 so png_handle_sCAL takes the `length < 4` true branch at pngrutil.c:2403.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds (vpc/cmplog) reach line 2403 and take its true side (W: T=18 F=15) and the blocker becomes hit (avg hitcount on branch winner=18 / 34), meaning the fuzzer produced an sCAL chunk with a length field <4; the I2S winners place the correct chunk-type literal bytes at the chunk position so the sCAL handler is dispatched at all.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds never take the true side (L: T=0 F=25) and the branch hitcount stays 0; without I2S the loser fails to synthesize the exact 4-byte sCAL chunk-type tag (and a sub-4 length) by random byte mutation, so png_handle_sCAL with length<4 is never dispatched.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc carry I2SRandReplace, which reads the CmpLogObserver record of the chunk-name comparison and substitutes the logged 'sCAL' FOURCC operand at the matching input offset, manufacturing the chunk-type literal that random havoc bytes (naive/value_profile) cannot place; once dispatched, a small/absent length field satisfies length<4.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the true side of `else if (length < 4)` inside png_handle_sCAL, the globally blocked side.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
Source line:    else if (length < 4)
Globally blocked side: T  (true branch)

evidence_trail.claim: Reaching the blocker requires the sCAL chunk handler to be entered after IHDR and before IDAT.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: /* Need unit type, width, \0, height: minimum 4 bytes */

evidence_trail.claim: Winner inputs take the true side of L2403 while loser inputs never do.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L2403  T=18 F=15  T=0 F=25  else if (length < 4)  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Both decisive pairs carry the I2S technique delta and the winners resolve the branch while losers block.
The winner produces hits on the branch where the loser produces none.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=18  loser=0

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog/vpc add I2SRandReplace, which substitutes a logged CMP operand at the matching input offset.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile / naive have no I2SRandReplace mutator, so they cannot perform the operand substitution.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating only on a length<4 integer comparison with NO preceding multi-byte literal chunk-type FOURCC match would show naive/value_profile resolving it as fast as cmplog/vpc (no I2S advantage), refuting that the divergence comes from I2S substitution of the chunk-type literal.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The seed byte dumps only show the first 0x40 bytes (PNG header/gAMA region), so I cannot directly point at the sCAL chunk-type tag or its sub-4 length field in the input — the FOURCC-substitution claim rests on the source dispatch structure plus the I2S-floor lineage rather than visible chunk bytes.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver entry for the chunk-type-vs-'tIME' comparison and substitutes the 'tIME' FOURCC into the input at the matching offset, routing into png_handle_tIME; naive and value_profile fix their chunk-type bytes at non-tIME values and never enter the handler.
signature.one_line: I2S places tIME FOURCC to dispatch handler where length!=7 inequality fires

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must contain a tIME chunk whose 4-byte type fourcc dispatches png_handle_tIME with a declared length other than 7

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A PNG chunk whose 4-byte type field equals the literal fourcc 'tIME' (74 49 4d 45), so the chunk dispatcher routes into OSS_FUZZ_png_handle_tIME, with the chunk's 4-byte big-endian declared length != 7 so line 2490 takes the true branch.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winning cmplog/vpc seeds reach OSS_FUZZ_png_handle_tIME and hit line 2490's true side (W: T=56 F=3), and the byte-diff chunk-type region (0x35-0x38) shows W carrying varied chunk fourccs (74 't', 52 'R', etc.) that I2SRandReplace can splice exact known fourccs into; the chunk-dispatch CMP against 'tIME' is solved by substitution rather than blind search.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Naive/value_profile losers never take line 2490's true branch (L: T=0 F=36) and never invoke png_handle_tIME on a non-7-length tIME chunk; without I2S they cannot reliably stamp the exact 'tIME' fourcc, so their chunk-type bytes at 0x35-0x38 stay fixed at a single value (52(R)x27, 47(G)x27, 42(B)x27).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver entry for the chunk-type-vs-'tIME' comparison and substitutes the recorded operand into the input at the matching offset, manufacturing the exact fourcc that routes to png_handle_tIME; naive/value_profile lack I2SRandReplace so cannot perform this targeted operand substitution.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the conditional length != 7 inside OSS_FUZZ_png_handle_tIME and the globally blocked side is the true branch.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
Source line:    if (length != 7)
Globally blocked side: T  (true branch)

evidence_trail.claim: Reaching this branch requires entering png_handle_tIME, i.e. the chunk dispatcher must route a tIME chunk.
Winners hit the true branch of line 2490 while losers never do.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L2490  T=56 F=3  T=0 F=36  if (length != 7)  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The chunk-type fourcc region of the input diverges between winners and losers, with losers fixed at a single value.
cmplog and vpc add I2SRandReplace which substitutes a recorded CMP operand into the input at matching offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace.
naive_ctx's CtxHook folds the call context into the edge-map index so the same edge counts at different slots per call path.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
The hook
keeps a running hash of the current call context (caller chain) and
folds it into the edge-map index, so the same basic-block edge is
recorded at different map slots depending on the call path that
reached it.

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx treats a new (call-context, edge) pair as new coverage, unlike plain naive.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
a "new bucket" is a new
(call-context, edge) pair rather than a bare edge.

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx wins the pair 10/10 while naive loses, blocked for 19.50h.
The true-branch body (2491-2495) fired only in the winner role per the coverage overlay.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness with a tIME-style chunk whose 4-byte type fourcc is checked by an exact equality CMP shows no cmplog/vpc advantage over naive/value_profile at reaching the non-7-length branch (equal resolve rates), refuting the I2S-fourcc-substitution claim.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The I2S target offset is inferred to be the chunk-type fourcc rather than the length field; the byte diff shows fourcc-region divergence but does not directly prove I2S spliced 'tIME' versus solving the length compare.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace substitutes the logged 'iTXt' FOURCC into the chunk-type field, routing execution into the iTXt handler where the keyword-length range check fires; naive's blind havoc never reliably produces the exact 4-byte tag.
signature.one_line: I2S plants 'iTXt' FOURCC enabling keyword prefix-length range check

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must carry a valid iTXt chunk whose keyword prefix length is 0 or >79 bytes to take the bad-keyword branch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A chunk whose 4-byte type field equals 'iTXt' (69 54 58 74) so png_handle_iTXt is dispatched, followed by a keyword region whose NUL-terminated prefix_length falls outside [1,79] (length 0 or >79), satisfying prefix_length > 79 || prefix_length < 1.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner cmplog seeds carry the literal 'iTXt' chunk-type tag at the chunk-type offsets (0x25-0x28 shows 69 54 58 74 'iTXt' in half the winners; seed 4 has 69 54 58 74 at 0x35-0x38 'iTXt') so the iTXt handler runs and the keyword-scan loop at L2761-2763 yields a prefix_length outside [1,79], driving the true side (W-only line 2768 errmsg = bad keyword).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser naive seeds keep the chunk type as 'gAMA'/'sRGB' (offset 0x25-0x28 = 67 41 4d 41 'gAMA' only, never the 'iTXt' value), so png_handle_iTXt is rarely entered with a prefix_length outside [1,79]; the blocker shows L taking only the false side (L2767 T=0 F=45).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the CMP buffer of the chunk-type dispatch and 4-byte comparisons, then substitutes the logged 'iTXt' FOURCC into the input chunk-type field, manufacturing the iTXt-dispatch + degenerate-keyword input that naive's blind havoc only stumbles on once in ten trials.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the bad-keyword length check whose true side requires prefix_length outside [1,79].
prefix_length is computed by scanning the keyword bytes up to the first NUL within the chunk.
Winner seeds carry the literal iTXt chunk-type tag that loser seeds lack at the chunk-type offsets.
A winner seed contains the iTXt FOURCC bytes 69 54 58 74 in its payload.
Loser naive seeds keep gAMA at the chunk-type offset, never the iTXt value.
At the blocker the loser only ever takes the false side while the winner takes the true side.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: L2767  T=16 F=7  T=0 F=45  if (prefix_length > 79 || prefix_length < 1)

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which substitutes a logged CMP operand at matching input offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog resolved the branch in all 10 trials while naive resolved in only 1.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where naive reaches the prefix_length>79||<1 branch as often as cmplog once the iTXt chunk-type FOURCC is fixed/pre-supplied, showing the divergence was not the iTXt-dispatch substitution.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The byte-diff shows iTXt FOURCC presence in winners but does not directly prove the keyword prefix_length itself was set outside [1,79] by I2S rather than by the keyword-scan loop landing there incidentally.
